lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... so Ava's devices can retrieve ammunition for me when I'm ranging, using the power of the magnet and the undead chicken.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: A magnet and an undead chicken? Really?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's useful.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... and the Dorgeshuun city is now connected to Keldagrim by a rapid train line.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: I am always glad to hear tales of the Dorgeshuun.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... and with the device destroyed, the people of Arceuus were safe.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... at the end of the day, I wonder what happened to Fox's equipment...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... after Willow betrayed me, I managed to defeat the guardian. Then I found the Imcando city of Camdozaal!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... but Pharaoh Kemesis agreed to begin preparations to reopen Menaphos.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... I had to be shot out of a cannon into a rock! But finally I banished the spirit of the rock and Dondakan could mine his gold.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... Poor Rantz was so clumsy he couldn't shoot anything! But I managed to kill the Chompy Bird.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... So it turned out there was no plague after all!

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: Deception of the people by their rulers is a terrible thing.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... So in the end the Black Knights were defeated by cabbage!

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: One should never underestimate the uses of the vegetables of Guthix.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... and that's how I got the archaeologists to Fossil Island!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... So I holed the enemy ship with another cannon shot, and the Adventurous sailed triumphantly into Mos Le'Harmless!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... and that's how I stopped the murder of the Teokan of Ralos!

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: I have no knowledge of this Ralos you speak of. How curious...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... but Veos had no idea what was going on. He'd been controlled remotely by his mysterious client the whole time!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... So it was I who repaired the clock tower.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... so I told Larry about the penguins' plot!

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: The paranoia and ambition of the penguins' leaders will not serve their people well.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... and in the end I found all the ingredients, so the Duke of Lumbridge had a birthday cake after all.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: Ah, a happy ending. It would not be good for such an anniversary to go unmarked.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... and after I'd defeated the giant scarab, the High Priest gave me a reward lamp to teach me more combat skills.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... where Captain Tock thanked me profusely for solving the mysterious 'curse' and killing the traitor.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... But in the end I stopped Fenkenstrain from continuing his horrible experiments.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: With the power to create life comes responsibility. I see that Fenkenstrain was not up to the task.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... and that's how I was able to deal with the questionable politics of Catherby.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: It seems you humans are always coming up with new political systems. This one sounds like it might need a bit more work...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... and so I escaped with the heart of Arrav!

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: The separation of heart from man is not natural. It is good you are looking to resolve such matters.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... I gave Safalaan the book on Haemalchemy, and he recommended me for a position on the Myreque.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: I am sure you will have many more adventures on behalf of the Myreque. Then, perhaps, you will have even more stories to tell me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... So from then on the Imperial Guard was able to access Death Plateau safely and take the trolls by surprise.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... Sigmund escaped again, but Zanik and I destroyed the machine and Dorgesh-Kaan was saved!

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: Zanik still has her destiny to fulfil, and I have a feeling you will have a part in that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... and so that's how I solved the case of the murder on the isle.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: Ambition is so often the death of reason. It is good you were able to find the culprit.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... the Shield of Arrav glowed with lightning, and Dimintheis used it to defeat the zombie army!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... So I destroyed the demon Delrith and saved Varrock!

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: I remember Delrith. A most unpleasant character; I am glad he has been dispatched.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... and I returned the Royal Accord of Twill to Lord Hosidius.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... In the pyramid I discovered a whole new set of magic spells!

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: I suggest you think about the origin of those spells. Ancient magics are not to be toyed with.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... and it turns out the treasure in the vault was actually the Elder Horn!

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: For an artefact like that to be unleashed upon this world... Let us hope this is not the end of the story.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... So it was me who accidentally brought a teleport beacon onto Entrana! But the Temple Knights are investigating so it should all be all right.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... and the examiner was very impressed that I had discovered an ancient altar of Zaros.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: Zaros? I had not heard that name for a thousand years even before the start of my sojourn here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... So once I had got all the ores he wanted, Doric let me use his anvils.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: Such a small task hardly seems worthy of the term 'quest'.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... So with Elvarg the dragon dead, the master of the Champions' Guild let me in, and I was able to wear Rune Plate!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... so I slew the dragon which killed the dragonkin as well.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not everyone survived the fight though.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: The dragonkin once brought this world to the brink of ruin. It is good you stopped them from doing so again.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... so Cyrisus was free of his fear of combat, and he headed off to be an adventurer.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: Fear is the most insidious enemy of all.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... So Kaqemeex taught me how to use the Herblore skill.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: A generous reward indeed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... and that was how I fixed the Dwarf multicannon.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: So war still rages in the world above? Will you never tire of creating machines of destruction?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... Eadgar's ruse was absurd, but it worked! The trolls ate a fake human, and I got the herb for the druids' ritual.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... so Nickolaus taught me how to catch a new ferret for the zoo.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... and once I had repaired the Elemental Workshop I was able to make an Elemental Shield.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... so I instilled the power of my mind into the primed bar and smithed it into an Elemental Mind Helm!

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: You should be careful about meddling with your own head.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... and then Akthanakos and Enakhra went away to the north to continue fighting!

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: A battle between two such powerful beings will take much time and unleash much energy. I hope that the natural world is not damaged in this conflict!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... so we landed the balloon in Taverley, and Auguste thanked me for helping him get his idea off the ground.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... So once I had found all the parts for the machine, poor Ernest could be himself once more.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: That was a good deed. It is a terrible thing to be locked out of one's natural form.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... and I know they meant well, but I did have to explain to them that they couldn't just go 'borrowing' from other museums!

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: Is it ever right to steal? It is a conundrum older than time, though I think in this case, the answer is clear.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... the machine revealed that the cute creatures were actually Arposandran spies! I destroyed them, and King Narnode gave me a crystal seed that belonged to Oaknock the Engineer.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... So I defeated the tanglefoot and returned the Fairy Queen's Enchanted Secateurs to the Fairy Godfather.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: These fairies seem to be well versed in the powers of nature.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... the Fairy Queen awoke, and we realised that the Godfather had betrayed her!

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: Politics is never straightforward.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... So all three parts of the family crest were reunited.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ...So despite the failure of my original task, Ali Morrisane was very happy about his nephew's fortunes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... and I defeated General Khazard, and won the Servils their freedom!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... and so it turned out that the archive was never completed. Everything Metzli did was for nothing...

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: She let her greed consume her, and paid the ultimate price.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... and after I had won the fishing contest, the Dwarves let me go under White Wolf Mountain.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: Fishing? A strange test of worthiness to pass through an underground tunnel!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... I think I did something with a drunken dwarf... but I can't remember what.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: You must be careful not to let drink muddle your brain!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... and so I took the hammer to Undor and he used it to repair the Doors of Dinh.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... and that's how we defeated the Jormungand once and for all!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... so I presented the head of the Troll King to the Burgher and he gave me his own helm!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... and that was how I became an honourary member the Fremennik, and was given my Fremennik name, [Fremennik name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... so it seems that in trying to prevent it, the Old Ones managed to cause their extinction instead.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... So the garden at Varrock palace is all my work!

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: It is good that you can take time from slaying and adventuring to grow a beautiful garden.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ...I returned Fluffs safely to Gertrude, and then she gave me a cat of my own!

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: Cats are one of the most mysterious creatures of Guthix. I hope you take your responsibility seriously.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... and so I ended up having to make a pretend head. Not exactly how I expected things to go, but at least I managed to keep my head up throughout it all!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... So the people of Port Phasmatys were finally able to rest.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... So the statue in Keldagrim was rebuilt, and I witnessed a meeting of the Consortium of mining companies.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: I have a feeling that Keldagrim will be the setting of many great adventures for you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... So the goblins ended up wearing the armour colour they had to start off with!

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: Poor silly goblins! Their race had such potential, if only they could rise above their petty squabbles.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... and I had to reprogram the golem before it would believe that the demon was dead.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: I remember well the battle of Uzer.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... Glough fled like the coward he really is, and the Grand Tree was saved!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... so I defeated Mi-Gor's barrel-chested bodyguard, and the monks were saved.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... and once I'd chopped down the beanstalk, Sylas let me keep Rupert's helmet.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: Your tales are often very curious, [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... So that was how I gave Bert a hand solving the murder and returned the wizard's head to solve the riddle of the ever full sand pits!

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: A victim dies and a murderer is punished. That is balance.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... and from the legendary crystals I cut a salve shard to fight the undead.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... So that was how I foiled the cultists in their plan to resurrect Hazeel.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... So that was how I helped return Hazeel to his followers.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: So the vile Mahjarrat still walk the earth above? Then Zamorak's defeat was not as thorough as I had thought.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... but it all went wrong and Itzla was captured!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... So after I had retrieved all the items, I became a member of the Heroes' Guild!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... and out of all the Knights of the Round Table, it was I who found the Holy Grail.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... and that was the end of the horror from the deep!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... and with the amulet I received, I could understand the language of cats!

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: It is a rare privilege to understand the language of those beings. You should listen carefully to all that they say - but not believe too much of it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... and I was rewarded for all my help.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... It took some time, but I finally got all four beads back, and Mizgog gave me my reward.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: Imps! I remember the age of great war, when armies of Zamorak's imps bloodied the ankles of the other gods' creatures.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... Veliaf was very impressed by the 'Rod of Ivandis', and I can use it to kill Juves and the Juvinates!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... So I suppose it was my fault that the young members of the Myreque were killed by Vanstrom.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... and once I had gathered all the herbs, Trufitus Shakaya was able to commune with his gods.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... and that's how Kourend came to be ruled by a king again. It just wasn't quite the king we initially expected.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... meanwhile Merlin used his magic to eject the Sinclairs from Camelot. King Arthur rewarded me for saving him.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... So that was how I found the Imcando Dwarves and got the Knight a new sword.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... Oldak and I escaped from Yu'biusk before the portal closed, but Zanik was trapped in the box.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... and when I had completed all the tasks, I became a member of the Legends' Guild!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... and when I entered the door carrying the Dramen Staff, I was transported to a whole new world -- a world populated by magical fairies!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... So Sigmund was dismissed, and the duke and ruler of the cave goblins signed a peace treaty.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: The Dorgeshuun goblins have been good neighbors during my vigil here. They are a timid race, but not cowardly, and I am glad they have the peace they desire.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... so I defeated my mirror image, won the respect of the Moon Clan and persuaded them to communicate with the Fremenniks!

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: The Moon Clan's reliance on magic is no more balanced than the Fremennik's hatred of it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... while Snowflake and My Arm live happily together in Weiss.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... so that's how the Outpost was saved and converted into a museum.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: By remembering the past, one should be able to avoid repeating its mistakes. Yet the mortal races show no sign of abandoning their obsession with war and bloodshed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... and that's how we brought kebabs to Varlamore!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... and when I told King Arthur that I had single-handedly freed Merlin from his crystal prison, he made me a Knight of the Round Table!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... but I was able to stab the killer with her own knife!

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: A masked killer slashing vulnerable young people in a deserted mansion? Is that what stories have become in this day and age? How formulaic!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... and the monks threw a big birthday party for the child, with lots of wine.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: I hope they supplied something more suitable for the child to drink.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... so I defeated the Jungle Demon and stopped the Monkeys' plot to take over Karamja.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: I see they couldn't make a monkey out of you!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, actually, I was a monkey for a bit.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... so I defeated Glough in his mutant form and prevented a war. King Narnode Shareen & King Awowogei signed a peace treaty.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: Glough's evil plans always threaten to upset the balance of our world. I am pleased you were able to stop him.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... I was too late to save the chieftain's daughter, but at least I gave her a proper burial.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: Brief is life, but more precious if its passing is properly honoured.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... So that's how I found out about the lost temple in the mountains that the mourners were searching for.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... What kind of twisted mind would come up with the light-beam puzzle in that temple?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... and that's how I solved the murder of Lord Sinclair.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... My Arm is still up on that rooftop, growing goutweed in his little farming patch!

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: It is good that a troll wishes to study the complex skill that is Farming.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... My Arm carried on growing goutweed in his little farming patch on that rooftop, until Drunken Dwarf's Leg took over.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: The names of trolls never cease to amaze me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... thus Filliman Tarlock became a Nature Spirit!

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: That is a fitting reward for a dedicated servant of Guthix. Thank you for helping him.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... So that was how I helped Filliman Tarlock transform into a Nature Spirit.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: It is good that you have helped a servant of Guthix to his rest.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... and so it turned out it wasn't actually Verzik at all. It was a nylocas!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... and when I had fixed the telescope, I looked through and saw the stars.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: It is long since I have seen the stars...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... where I found a note from Ulfric Longbeard and some treasure!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... I still can't believe how long that 'one small favour' took me! I ended up walking all over the world doing small favours for all and sundry!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... so even though Will and Anne got away, at least I survived the ordeal.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... the room filled up with gas and I nearly died, but Hazelmere came to my rescue.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: A truly exciting tail of failing and needing to be rescued.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... and whilst we managed to reset the seal, Eyatlalli still needs my help to keep the Moons of Peril in check.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: A nagua? It has been a long time since I last heard of their kind...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... and when I dug in the middle of the park in Falador, I found the pirate's treasure!

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: Such is ever the folly of pirates, the bury their loot in the ground so that another can dig it up.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... and that was how I rescued Elena from West Ardougne.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... but using my new goggles, I was able to avoid the acidic attack of the Sourhog and I came out victorious.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... But with Drezel's help I was able to purify the Salve.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... and I had to disguise Prince Ali in order to smuggle him out!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... so I guess I'll never be short on crowbars again.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... so Councillor Hughes was arrested, and Lady Piscarilius rewarded me for my help.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... So I fetched all the bones the Odd Old Man wanted, and he gave me a reward!

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: What could that man have wanted all those bones for? Collecting that many bones cannot be a balanced activity.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... So I fetched all the bones on the old man's wish-list, as well as the ones I'd brought him earlier.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... Those rat-catchers are odd people. But by the end of it I had caught a huge number of rats all over Gielinor!

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: Rats are a highly unbalanced creature and it is right that their numbers be controlled.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... So after I had freed all the dinner guests I was finally able to face the Culinaromancer and put an end to his mad schemes!

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: That evil wizard's time spells sent a ripple through the fabric of balance. I am glad he is no longer at large!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... So I figured out their weird puzzles, and the Temple Knights asked me to join as an initiate member!

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: I have heard tales of these 'Temple Knights' from many years past. Be wary, their motivations are not what they might lead you to believe.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... I returned to tell King Lathas that the assassination was successful, and he rewarded me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... and that was how I negotiated peace between frogs and humans.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: Forgive me, [player name], but that story was quite... unusual.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... and once I returned the skull, the ghost was able to rest.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: A strange attachment to an item that has no use after one's death.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... I gave the message to Romeo, but he misunderstood, so they never were together.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: Never was a story of more woe That this of Juliet and her Romeo.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... and when I planted the shard, it grew into a crystal tree!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... So I showed that the war was based on a misunderstanding, and now Miscellania and Etceteria were at peace again!

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: War? What is it good for?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Absolutely nothing!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... So that was how I helped keep the zombie pirates drunk!

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: Drunken zombie pirates? What is the world coming to?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... So I brought Aubury's notes to Sedridor the Head Wizard, and from then on I was able to mine Rune Essence.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: So the mortals above have discovered magic once more? Very interesting.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... I would never have thought that three scorpions would take that long to find!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... Alan was convinced by the new egg, but he then may have had a little accident himself...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... and that was how I rescued Caroline's family from the Sea Slugs.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... and so the assassin captured Jhallan and teleported him away.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: Be very careful of the Mahjarrat, [player name]. None of them are to be trusted.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... So Mort'ton returned pretty much to normal, and I was able to put the shades to rest.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... So Denath was Agrith-Naar all along! But I managed to summon the demon again and slay him.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: Agrith-Naar was one of the demons my master banished at the end of the great war. It seems he found a way to work around the ban on direct intervention.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... and that was how I saved the townsfolk from the custodians!

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: Ever curious, the continued evolution of creatures, isn't it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... and I incinerated the bones of the sheep in the furnace.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... Shearing all those sheep was hard work, but the pay was good!

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: Nature gave sheep their wool for a reason, you know. I hope they were not too cold.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... So that's how I helped return the Shield of Arrav.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... So what Rashiliyia wanted all along was to be reunited with her son in the afterlife.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... but Lord Drakan was too strong, and we were unable to defeat him. The Myreque were defeated and Safalaan was taken!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... and once I finished making Kovac his sword, he gave me full access to the Foundry

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... when I told Sir Tiffy that I had accidentally freed the Mother Mallum, he promoted me to the rank of Proselyte within the Temple Knights.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: That was a strange response to such an unfortunate event. But the motives of the Temple Knights have always been incomprehensibly unbalanced.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... and so the Iorwerth elves were finally defeated and the clan elders restored the city of Prifddinas!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ...So I defeated all the monsters in Tolna's subconscious and he escaped from the rift.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: The most fearsome monsters are to be found in the darkness of our own minds.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... So it turned out that the people of Nardah were dying of thirst because the spirits of the Elid had cursed them for turning away from their goddess.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: It is a terrible thing for people to turn away from their religion. I think the spirits taught the town a lesson.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... So the Wise Old Man said it was time for him to retire, and he left the Colony and went home. He gave me loads of XP, but he wouldn't let me have his hat!

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: A hat is just a hat, [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Grrr!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... So that was how I helped the people of Tai Bwo Wannai village return.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ...now Bob and Neite are very happy together!

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: Bob the Cat? I have heard of that strange creature but have never been able to fathom him. It is no small achievement for you to be able to!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... So Bob and Neite were very happy together, at least for a while.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: The loss of a friend is never easy. However, you should always remember to be thankful of the time you had together.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... and Lord Shayzien ordered an investigation into the disappearance of Phileas.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... and Lord Shayzien ordered an investigation into the disappearance of Phileas, but in the end, it turned out he was the one responsible!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... and Safalaan rewarded me for defeating Ranis and bringing a true taste of hope to the people of Meiyerditch.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... so you said you'd look after my bowl for me, and let me access the Tears if I kept telling you stories.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: I knew that story already, in fact, but I suppose your own perspective of the events is a valid tale in itself.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... luckily for us, the Guardians of the Rift are now working to keep the Abyss at bay. I just hope we can find out how to close the rift permanently.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... I killed Lucien and banished him from the world!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... Lucien said that the Staff of Armadyl I gave him had made him more powerful.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... So that was how I became [King/Queen] of Miscellania!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... Ana wasn't too happy to be cooped up in that barrel! But at least I got her out of the mining camp.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... and the homunculus now lives in the basement of the Tower, creating living creatures of its own.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: The alchemists walked a dangerous path when they created that homunculus. I am glad that events ended well for the creature.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... and King Bolren thanked me for defeating the warlord and returning the orbs to the gnome people.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... So I returned the totem, and the Rantuki tribe rewarded me for my help.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... So Ug and Aga lived happily ever after.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... and after I had rescued Godric, Dunstan gave me the Law Talisman as a reward!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... and that was how I helped the tortugans of the Great Conch deal with their gryphon problem.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... so I was able to defend the Teomat, but someone attacked the Teokan anyway!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... I don't know who put all those traps in the underground pass!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... and once the vampyre was dead, the people of Draynor no longer lived in fear.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... and after I finally managed to track him down, let me tell you, that Solus Dellagar fellow was almost as powerful as he was insane!

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: Yes, it seems he sacrificed his sanity for power. Power often comes with a cost, and the highest cost is often the one you do not immediately see...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... and with the shield generator working, the ogres could never threaten Yanille again.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... and that was how I retrieved the treasure from the waterfall.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... so King Roald was freed from Surok's spell, and Rat thanked me for all my help.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... but Lucien took the Stone of Jas and teleported away!

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: The last time a Mahjarrat was in possession of the Stone, this world saw destruction unlike anything imaginable. Still, good may yet come of this. We shall see.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... All that trouble just to get a ball out of someone's garden!

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: It is often hard to know how long a task will take when we begin it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... and once I got her all the ingredients, Hetty's potion increased my magical power!

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: I see you are on your way to becoming strong in the magical arts.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... and in the end I managed to solve the clues and find the treasure for Veos.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... Those Zogres were disgusting! But I learned how to fight them with brutal arrows and disease balm.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: Zombie Ogres!? Some of the foulest creatures of Zamorak. In the great wars the zogre armies could repel the forces of Saradomin with their stench alone!

sections.title: Talking to her before making the stone bowl

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: Before you can collect the Tears of Guthix you must make a bowl out of the stone in the cave on the south of the chasm.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But I don't know how to reach the cave!

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: I will tell you the story of the light-creatures.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: Myriad and beautiful were the creatures and civilizations of the early ages of the world. Gielinor was a work of art, shaped lovingly over the millennia by the creative mind of Guthix.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: Only the sturdiest races survived the Godwars, and even then only by abandoning their high culture and gearing their societies towards war. Of the more delicate races there is now no trace, and almost no memory.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: One such race had bodies as fragile as snowflakes, yet they built crystal cities that stood for a thousand years.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: The wind would whisper through the spires and fill them with sweet harmonies, and the rising sun would shine through the precious gems that studded the towers and create inter plays of light as if rainbows were dancing.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: Indeed, so marvellous was this light-show at its height that the patterns of light themselves became alive, and great flocks of luminous creatures rode along the gem- cast beams, each drawn to its own colour.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: The creatures you see floating in this chasm are the last sorry remnants of that age. I do not know how they made their way here and survived to this time, but I am grateful for their company.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay.

sections.title: Using the magic stone on Juna

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: Perhaps you should use it with a chisel, rather than with my face.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: Tell me... a story...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay...

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: Your stories have entertained me. I will let you into the cave for a short time.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: But first you will need to make a bowl in which to collect the tears.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: There is a cave on the south side of the chasm that is similarly infused with the power of Guthix. The stone in that cave is the only substance that can catch the Tears of Guthix.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: Mine some stone from that cave, make it into a bowl, and bring it to me, and then I will let you catch the Tears.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, I don't really have one.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: Hmm. Maybe you should come back when you do.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A story?

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: I have been waiting here three thousand years, guarding the Tears of Guthix. I serve my master faithfully, but I am bored.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: An adventurer such as yourself must have many tales to tell. If you can entertain me, I will let you into the cave for a time.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: The more I enjoy your story, the more time I will give you in the cave.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: Then you can drink of the power of balance, which will make you stronger in whatever area you are weakest.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are the Tears of Guthix?

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: The Third Age of the world was a time of great conflict, of destruction never seen before or since, when all the gods save Guthix warred for control.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: The colossal Wyrms, of whom today's dragons are a pale reflection, turned all the sky to fire, while on the ground armies of foot soldiers, goblins and trolls and humans, filled the valleys and plains with blood.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: In time the noise of the conflict woke Guthix from His deep slumber, and He rose and stood in the centre of the battlefield so that the splendour of His wrath filled the world, and He called for the conflict to cease!

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: Silence fell, for the gods knew that none could challenge the power of the mighty Guthix -- for His power is that of nature itself, to which all other things are subject, in the end.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: Guthix reclaimed that which had been stolen from Him, and went back underground to return to His sleep and continue to draw the world's power into Himself.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: But on His way into the depths of the earth He sat and rested in this cave; and, thinking of the battle-scarred desert that now stretched from one side of His world to the other, He wept.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: And so great was His sorrow, and so great was His life- giving power, that the rocks themselves began to weep with Him.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: Later, Guthix noticed that the rocks continued to weep, and that their tears were infused with a small part of His power.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: So He set me, His servant, to guard the cave, and He entrusted to me the task of judging who was and was not worthy to access the tears.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: Tell me... a story...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You tell me a story.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about the Tears of Guthix.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: The Third Age of the world was a time of great conflict, of destruction never seen before or since, when all the gods save Guthix warred for control.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: The colossal Wyrms, of whom today's dragons are a pale reflection, turned all the sky to fire, while on the ground armies of foot soldiers, goblins and trolls and humans, filled the valleys and plains with blood.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: In time the noise of the conflict woke Guthix from His deep slumber, and He rose and stood in the centre of the battlefield so that the splendour of His wrath filled the world, and He called for the conflict to cease!

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: Silence fell, for the gods knew that none could challenge the power of the mighty Guthix -- for His power is that of nature itself, to which all other things are subject, in the end.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: Guthix reclaimed that which had been stolen from Him, and went back underground to return to His sleep and continue to draw the world's power into Himself.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: But on His way into the depths of the earth He sat and rested in this cave; and, thinking of the battle-scarred desert that now stretched from one side of His world to the other, He wept.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: And so great was His sorrow, and so great was His life- giving power, that the rocks themselves began to weep with Him.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: Later, Guthix noticed that the rocks continued to weep, and that their tears were infused with a small part of His power.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: So He set me, His servant, to guard the cave, and He entrusted to me the task of judging who was and was not worthy to access the tears.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: Tell me... a story...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me a new story.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: I have already told you my story, and you have not told me yours!

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: Tell me... a story...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not now.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: Before you can collect the Tears of Guthix you must make a bowl out of the stone in the cave on the south of the chasm.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have a bowl.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: I will keep your bowl for you, so that you may collect the tears many times in the future.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: Now... tell me another story, and I will let you collect the tears for the first time.
